nativeRange.habitat: Coral reef lagoons and reef flats at depths of 3–15 m, typically found among the branches of live Pocillopora and Seriatopora stony corals

diet.notes: Feeds primarily on zooplankton and coral mucus in nature. In captivity, tends to accept enriched Mysis shrimp and small frozen/meaty foods; targeted feeding near the coral host is generally recommended as this fish is a small, cryptic feeder.

summary: A tiny (max ~3.5 cm) Indo-Pacific coral goby with a distinctive red head and black body, generally considered reef-safe and peaceful; tends to associate closely with branching Pocillopora and Seriatopora corals in the wild and is rarely available in the trade.
careNotes: The Warthead Goby is an obligate coral-dweller associated with branching Pocillopora and Seriatopora stony corals in the wild, where it finds food, shelter, and breeding sites. Specimens added to reef aquariums generally do best in tanks with established colonies of these corals. This species is not routinely collected for the aquarium trade; husbandry is generally considered similar to other Paragobiodon gobies such as P. lacunicolus. A tight-fitting lid is generally advisable as gobies have been known to jump. Temperature sources disagreed: LiveAquaria listed 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) while FishBase field data gives 25–29.3°C (mean 28.3°C in native habitat); the published values have been averaged to 23.5–27.5°C. Hobbyists keeping this species near the lower LiveAquaria range may find it overlaps with typical reef temperatures; the FishBase range reflects wild tropical Indo-Pacific conditions and may be more representative of optimal long-term keeping temperatures. Care level is rated intermediate primarily due to the species' dependence on live stony corals for optimal health and its rarity in the trade rather than water-parameter sensitivity.
breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity has not been well-documented in available sources. The species has been reported to lay eggs on its coral host in the wild. Detailed spawning and rearing information is not currently available.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Product page with full care parameters including temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.023–1.025), tank size (25 gal), care level (Easy), diet and behavior.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (3.5 cm TL confirmed), native range (Indo-Pacific: East Africa to Marshall Islands), temperature (25–29.3°C field data). Temperature disagreed with LiveAquaria — averaged to 23.5–27.5°C. FishBase MNHN primary URL (https://fishbase.mnhn.fr/country/CountrySpeciesSummary.php?c_code=480&id=22477) returned HTTP 403; fishbase.se used as fallback secondary.

additional.name: Fishes of Australia
additional.notes: Confirmed obligate Pocillopora coral association, 3.5 cm TL max size, Indo-west-central Pacific range including Red Sea through to French Polynesia and eastern Australia.

fish.breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity is not documented in available sources. Eggs are deposited on coral substrate in the wild.
